easily-garlic-herb-roasted-potatoes.name: Easily Garlic Herb Roasted Potatoes
instructions: Fill a large pot with water and boil. Add salt, baking soda, and potatoes, and stir.

Return to a boil, reduce to a simmer, and cook for 10 minutes after returning to a boil. A fork should easily pierce the potato pieces by the end. Drain and let sit one minute while moisture evaporates.

Combine olive oil, rosemary (or thyme), garlic, and a few grinds of black pepper in a small saucepan and heat over medium heat. Stir constantly until garlic just begins to turn golden, about 2-3 minutes.
Immediately strain oil through a fine-mesh strainer over the potatoes. Add salt and pepper to taste, and mix.
Shake the pot that the potatoes are in so that they slam against all sides of the pot, this will loosen the outer layer of the potatoes to form a mashed layer around each potato piece.
Preheat oven to 218°C (220°C).
Transfer potatoes to a large rimmed baking sheet and separate them, spreading them out evenly.
Transfer to oven and roast, without moving, for 30-35 minutes.
Using a thin, flexible metal spatula flip potatoes. Continue roasting until potatoes are deep brown and crisp all over, turning and shaking them a few times during cooking, 30 to 40 minutes longer.
Transfer potatoes to a large bowl and add the garlic-rosemary mixture (optional) and minced parsley. Toss to coat and season with more salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

edamame-guacamole.name: Edamame Guacamole
instructions: BRING a medium pot of water to a boil. Prepare an ice bath and set aside.
COOK the edamame beans at a rolling boil until tender, 10 to 11 minutes. Drain the beans into a strainer and shock in the ice bath. Drain well.
PUREE the avocado, lime juice, garlic, and jalapeño in a food processor at high speed. Add the edamame beans and the process on high speed.
MEANWHILE, cook the broccoli at a rolling boil until tender, 7 to 8 minutes. Drain the broccoli and shock in the ice bath. Drain well and pat dry.
ADD the broccoli to the edamame mixture and process on high speed until very smooth, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed to incorporate all the ingredients.
TRANSFER the mixture to a medium bowl and fold in the remaining ingredients, mixing with a rubber spatula until well incorporated.

TRANSFER the guacamole to an airtight container and refrigerate until chilled, about 1 hour, before serving with accompaniment of choice. (The guacamole will keep in the refrigerator for up to two days.)

egg-fritters-with-toasted-bread-argiro.name: Egg Fritters With Toasted Bread

ingredients.item: 1 tb wet vanilla or 2 vanilla capsules
4-6 tabletspoons of chocolate or 125 g of grated chocolate of your choice
1 tb wet vanilla or 2 vanilla capsules
4-6 tabletspoons of chocolate or 125 g of grated chocolate of your choice

instructions: We're going to use a 23×33 cm long fireproof vessel.
In bowl, whisk the eggs, vanilla, salt and milk together.
Brush with butter the entire surface of the baking pan.
Sprinkle up the black sugar and cover the walls.
Spread 2-2 slices of bread together, making a thinkslayer on the basis of the baking pan and cut them lightly if necessary to fill the base and corners of the baking pan.
Pour slowly with 1–1/2 cup egg mixture – milk.
Add the Caramelized banners and cover with the ramine.
Cover the actors with the rest of the bread. The surface of the baking pan is covered with 6 whole slides and half slide cut in half.
We push slightly to stick the layerers together.
Pour with the remaining liquids (eggs – milk) and let it stand and suck for 30′′.
If you have time, the more you let it water the bread, the more fluffy and delicious the sweet will become.
Bake in a well preheated ocean at 170oC in the air, on the medium rack, for about 35′ until it is slightly browned and stable.
Leave for 10 minutes on the counter.
Cut into pieces and serve with some icing or pour with some honey.

eggless-sugar-cookies.name: Eggless Sugar Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.
Whisk both flours, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together in a medium bowl. Mix water and cornstarch together in a small bowl.

Combine butter and both sugars in a large bowl; beat with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy. Mix in cornstarch mixture, then add yogurt and vanilla and beat thoroughly. Add dry ingredients in small batches, beating well after each addition.
Drop tablespoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto the prepared baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven until edges are golden, 10 to 12 minutes. Cool on the baking sheet for 3 to 4 minutes before removing to a wire rack to cool completely, about 10 more minutes.

eggnog-with-bourbon-and-rum.name: Eggnog With Bourbon And Rum
instructions: Beat the egg yolks well until they turn light in color, adding 1/2 cup of the sugar as you beat. Add the milk, cream, and liquor. Then beat the egg whites with the remaining sugar until they peak. Fold the whites into the mixture. Grate the fresh nutmeg over the drink.

eggplant-and-red-onion-fritters-teleculi.name: Eggplant And Red Onion Fritters
eggplant-and-red-onion-fritters-teleculi.original_name: Pataniscas de beringela e cebola roxa
instructions: Place the eggs and flour in a bowl, season with salt and pepper and mix with a wire rod.
Peel the onion and pinch it finely. Wash the eggplant and cut it into cubes.
Add the eggplant and onion to the egg preparation, wrap well, then add chopped parsley to taste and mix again.
Spooned fry from the previous preparation in warm and abundant oil, then pours on absorbent paper and serves.

eggplant-meatball-basket-lezzet.name: Eggplant Meatball Basket
instructions: 1. For meatballs, soak bread and drink.
2. Peel onions and off.
3. A deep rough and planted onions, bread, eggs, pepper, kimyon, thyme and salt and knead.
4. Handle and roll the pieces in the size of Tangerine and shape the basket.
5. 10 minutes in the oven with a set of oiled tray and set to 200 degrees; cook.
6. Peel tomatoes and rub it in the form of dice and remove it.
7. Crush garlic.
8. Shake the eggplants in the oven and peel the shells.
9. Add and crush lemon juice, garlic, salt and yogurt.
10. Share the eggplant into the meatballs and sprinkle the tomatoes on them.
11. Cook in 200 grade oven.
12. Serve.

el-diablo.name: El Diablo
instructions: Combine tequila, lime juice, and crème de cassis in a highball glass. Fill glass with ice, then top off with ginger beer. Garnish with thyme sprig.

enchilada-baked-chicken-thighs.name: Enchilada Baked Chicken Thighs
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Combine chicken and 4 ounces enchilada sauce in a bowl; toss to combine. Transfer onto a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.

Remove baking sheet from the oven and drain liquid. Baste remaining enchilada sauce over chicken thighs and return to the oven. Continue baking until chicken is no longer pink in the center and the juices run clear, about 10 more minutes. Remove from oven and divide Cheddar cheese and olives evenly on top of the chicken.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Broil chicken until cheese is melted, about 5 minutes. Garnish with green onions, sour cream, and cilantro.

enchilada-stuffed-shells.name: Enchilada Stuffed Shells
instructions: Bring water to a boil. Cook the pasta shells until al dente. Drain, rinse with cold water, and set aside to cool.
Pour the oil in a pan, then add the onions and cook on medium heat until softened. Add the green chiles, chicken, and spices and stir to combine. Cook on low heat for an additional 3-4 minutes.
Place chicken mixture in a bowl. Add ricotta, queso fresco, and monterey jack and stir until all ingredients are well combined.
Stuff each shell with a large spoonful of the mixture.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190°C).
Coat the bottom of a baking dish with a thin layer of green enchilada sauce. Place the pasta shells in the dish in a single layer.
Cover with the rest of the enchilada sauce and some additional queso fresco and monterey jack.
Bake for 30 minutes (or until the cheese has melted and the sauce begins to bubble around the edges). Top with scallions.
Enjoy!

ever-irish-soda-bread.name: Ever Irish Soda Bread
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9-inch pan.

Stir flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, and baking soda together in a large bowl. Using a pastry cutter, cut the butter gently into the flour mixture until well combined; stir in raisins. Whisk buttermilk and eggs together in a separate bowl; lightly beat egg mixture into flour mixture. Place dough into the prepared pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until the bread has risen and the top is golden brown, 45 minutes to 1 hour. A knife inserted into the center of the bread should come out clean. Cool the bread in the pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes before removing. Serve warm.

ever-pina-colada-cake.name: Ever Piña Colada Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Spray a 9x13-inch baking pan with cooking spray.
Blend cake mix, water, oil, and egg whites in a bowl with an electric mixer until moistened. Pour batter into the prepared pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean, 23 to 28 minutes.
Remove from the oven and poke holes into the surface of the hot cake. Pour piña colada mix over cake, followed by condensed milk. Let cool completely, at least 2 hours.
Spread whipped topping over cake, then sprinkle with coconut. Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.
